summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a tight, spacey show featuring standout performances of Deal, I Need a Miracle and Bertha.
review: The recording is praised as a real‑board tape with crisp overall sound, though a few early mix issues and a taper drop‑off toward the end are noted. Musically the concert delivers spacey jams, tight playing and several “gold” moments—especially Deal → I Need a Miracle → Bertha—and Jerry’s guitar work receives particular acclaim.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance with multiple highlighted songs and solid playing, combined with generally good soundboard quality despite minor mix and taper fade‑out issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp real‑board sound praised; early set has bass low in the mix, later the taper’s quality fades toward the end.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Show starts strong, builds into a hot middle with spacey jamming and tight playing, then taper quality declines near the end.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry is described as shredding again – his guitar work stands out positively.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass was low in the mix at first but when it came up the whole band sounded better.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob delivered notable jazz‑run improvisations during the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drum segment is called “gold,” indicating a strong drum performance.
